meta.title: The Clever Monkey — Set 5
meta.subject: English Reading
meta.description: Challenge quiz for 'The Clever Monkey' — inference, theme, and advanced vocabulary.

questions.question: How many times did the monkey eat some cheese to 'fix' the pieces?
options: Once
Twice
Three or more times
He never ate any cheese
questions.explanation: The monkey said 'Uh-oh' multiple times and ate cheese each time. He kept eating until there were only two tiny pieces left.

questions.question: The monkey loved cheese, just like the cats.
questions.explanation: Yes! The story says the monkey 'also loved cheese more than just about anything.' This is why he wanted to steal the cats' cheese.

questions.question: Where was the monkey at the beginning of the story?
options: On the ground
In the water
In the tree above the cats
In a cave
questions.explanation: The story says 'In the tree above the two cats, a monkey was watching.' He saw everything from up in the tree.

questions.question: The cats 'could not believe their good _______.' They were very lucky to find the cheese.
questions.explanation: 'Fortune' means luck. 'Good fortune' means very good luck. The cats were surprised and happy to find the cheese.

questions.question: Why did the first cat say 'They don't have to be exactly the same size'?
options: The first cat was full and did not want more cheese
The first cat realized the monkey was tricking them and wanted to stop losing cheese
The first cat was being kind to the monkey
The first cat wanted to share with the monkey
questions.explanation: The first cat noticed the cheese was getting smaller and smaller. It wanted to stop the monkey from eating more by saying the pieces didn't need to be exactly equal.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: 'The clever monkey is still smiling.' This is from the very last line of the story — the monkey succeeded in his trick.

questions.question: Sort these sentences — are they TRUE or FALSE about the story?
items: The monkey lived in a tree
The cats divided the cheese themselves
The monkey ate much of the cheese

categories.label: True
answers: The monkey lived in a tree
The monkey ate much of the cheese

categories.label: False
answers: The cats divided the cheese themselves

questions.explanation: The cats found the cheese ✓. The monkey lived in a tree ✓. The monkey ate much of the cheese ✓. The cats did NOT divide it — the monkey did.

questions.question: The word 'jungle' refers to a thick tropical forest full of trees and animals.
questions.explanation: Yes! A 'jungle' is a dense tropical forest. The story is set 'in a jungle in West Africa' where many animals live.

questions.question: What is the best word to describe the monkey in this story?
options: Kind
Greedy and clever
Honest
Lazy
questions.explanation: The monkey was 'greedy' — he wanted cheese for himself. He was also 'clever' — he used the cats' argument to trick them into letting him eat all the cheese.

questions.question: The monkey's plan was very _______. He used the cats' argument to get the cheese.
questions.explanation: The monkey was both 'tricky' and 'clever.' He used the cats' mistrust to eat all their cheese without them realizing it until it was too late.

questions.question: What does the phrase 'For all we know, they are still there arguing' suggest?
options: The cats eventually found more cheese
The argument never ended — the cats never stopped disagreeing
The monkey came back to help the cats
The cats forgave each other and became friends
questions.explanation: This phrase is a humorous way to end the story, suggesting the cats' argument was endless. It shows that they never learned to trust each other — the real reason they lost their cheese.

questions.question: Arrange the words to make a sentence about the moral:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: 'Arguing can make you lose everything.' This is the lesson of the story — the cats lost almost all their cheese because they kept arguing.

questions.question: The second cat was right to insist on equal pieces, but both cats were wrong to let the monkey divide for so long.
questions.explanation: Both cats had some truth — wanting equal pieces is fair. But allowing the monkey to keep 'fixing' the pieces was a mistake. They should have stopped earlier, like the first cat suggested.

questions.question: The monkey 'swung back up into the tree.' What does this tell us about the monkey?
options: The monkey was angry
The monkey felt sorry for the cats
The monkey left happily with a full belly after his trick worked
The monkey was afraid of the cats
questions.explanation: Swinging back up into the tree shows the monkey left proudly and happily. He had succeeded in his trick and eaten most of the cheese. The story says he was 'still smiling.'

questions.question: Sort these vocabulary words from the story into their correct groups.
questions.explanation: 'Jungle' (a forest) and 'mistake' (an error) are nouns. 'Fairly' (in a fair way), 'sadly' (in a sad way), and 'exactly' (in an exact way) are adverbs.
